note: Canonical character roster for Larry McMurtry's Lonesome Dove (1985). Colors are ink colors used for journey trails.

characters.name: Capt. Woodrow F. Call
characters.role: Co-owner of the Hat Creek Cattle Company; legendary former Texas Ranger. Taciturn, relentless, married to work. Rides the Hell Bitch, a grey mare no one else can touch.
characters.fate: Drives the herd to Montana, then keeps his promise: hauls Gus's body 2,500 miles home to Texas. Never manages to tell Newt he is his father.

characters.name: Capt. Augustus McCrae
characters.role: Co-owner of the Hat Creek outfit; talker, philosopher, biscuit-maker, deadliest shot on the border. Author of the sign and its Latin motto. Still half in love with Clara Allen.
characters.fate: Takes two arrows in the legs north of the Yellowstone. Refuses the second amputation and dies in Miles City, Montana — "It's been quite a party, ain't it?"

characters.name: Newt Dobbs
characters.role: Seventeen, son of the late Maggie of Lonesome Dove — and, though never acknowledged, of Captain Call. The drive is his coming of age.
characters.fate: Left in charge of the Montana ranch. Call gives him the Hell Bitch, his rifle, and his father's watch — everything but the words.

characters.name: Joshua Deets
characters.role: Scout and tracker for the outfit, thirty years beside Call and Gus. Reads weather, water, and trouble better than any man on the plains. Wears patched quilt trousers.
characters.fate: Killed in Wyoming by a terrified young warrior while gently returning a blind Indian child. His grave board, carved by Call, reads: "Cherful in all weathers, never sherked a task. Splendid behaviour."

characters.name: Lorena Wood
characters.role: The only sporting woman in Lonesome Dove, dreaming of cool San Francisco. Leaves with Jake Spoon when the drive heads north.
characters.fate: Kidnapped by Blue Duck and sold to Kiowas; rescued by Gus, who becomes her shelter. Stays with Clara Allen on the Platte when the herd moves on.

characters.name: Jake Spoon
characters.role: Charming ex-Ranger, gambler, drifter — the man who plants the Montana idea. Accidentally killed a dentist in Fort Smith and has been running since.
characters.fate: Drifts into the murderous Suggs gang. Hanged in Kansas by his oldest friends. Spurs his own horse out from under himself.

characters.name: Clara Allen
characters.role: Gus's great lost love, sixteen years gone. Runs a horse ranch on the North Platte near Ogallala with two daughters and a husband dying of a horse kick.
characters.fate: Takes in Lorena, July Johnson, and Elmira's abandoned baby. Sends Call off with the hardest words anyone says to him in the whole book.

characters.name: July Johnson
characters.role: Young, dutiful sheriff of Fort Smith, Arkansas, sent after Jake Spoon when a stray shot kills July's own brother, the mayor. Quiet, decent, unlucky.
characters.fate: Loses everyone he travels with, then loses Elmira twice. Settles at Clara's ranch as a hand, hopelessly in her orbit.

characters.name: Elmira Boot Johnson
characters.role: July's restless wife, a former sporting woman. Slips away on a whiskey boat up the Arkansas the moment July leaves town, chasing her old flame Dee Boot.
characters.fate: Gives birth to July's son at Clara's and abandons him within days. Killed by Sioux raiders after fleeing Ogallala.

characters.name: Blue Duck
characters.role: Comanchero killer, half legend — the one man Gus and Call never caught in their rangering days. Steals Lorena off the plains almost as an afterthought.
characters.fate: Caught years of murders later; cheats the rope in Santa Rosa, New Mexico by riding a deputy through a third-floor courthouse window. Call witnesses it.

characters.name: Pea Eye Parker
characters.role: Loyal ex-Ranger corporal, slow of thought, sure of duty. Has followed the captains for thirty years without ever quite wondering why.
characters.fate: Escapes the Indian attack that dooms Gus by floating the river at night and walking the plains, naked and starving, until he finds the herd.

characters.name: Dish Boggett
characters.role: Top hand on the drive, point rider start to finish. Heart broken on a fixed star: Lorena.
characters.fate: Reaches Montana, then quits and rides all the way back to Nebraska to be near her. She never looks at him.

characters.name: Roscoe Brown
characters.role: July's hapless deputy, sent into Texas to tell July his wife has run off. The frontier is comprehensively unkind to him.
characters.fate: Murdered by Blue Duck on the Texas plains, along with the boy Joe and the girl Janey.

characters.name: Joe Boot
characters.role: Elmira's twelve-year-old son, brought along on July's manhunt because Elmira wanted him gone.
characters.fate: Killed by Blue Duck while the lawmen are off rescuing Lorena.

characters.name: Janey
characters.role: Feral, quick-witted runaway girl who attaches herself to Roscoe on the trail and saves his hide more than once.
characters.fate: Dies fighting Blue Duck with a rock in her hand.

characters.name: Sean O'Brien
characters.role: Young Irish immigrant swept up (with brother Allen) by the outfit during the Mexico raid. Sings to the cattle; homesick the whole way.
characters.fate: First death of the drive: swarmed by water moccasins crossing the Nueces.

characters.name: Bolivar
characters.role: The outfit's surly Mexican cook, a retired bandit who beats the dinner bell with a broken crowbar.
characters.fate: Turns back to his family early in the drive. Still in Lonesome Dove when Call returns.

characters.name: Po Campo
characters.role: Bolivar's replacement, hired by Call in San Antonio: a tiny, prophetic cook who walks the whole way to Montana, fries grasshoppers in molasses, and fears the Yellowstone.
characters.fate: Survives to keep the skillet warm at the Milk River ranch.

characters.name: Lippy Jones
characters.role: Piano player at the Dry Bean with a leaky hole in his stomach and a bowler hat. Begs onto the drive rather than be left behind.
characters.fate: Makes Montana alive — outliving men twice as able.

characters.name: Wilbarger
characters.role: Yale-educated cattleman with a taste for Milton, who buys the stolen Flores horses and keeps turning up like a dry-witted omen.
characters.fate: Ambushed and mortally wounded by Dan Suggs's gang; dies under Gus's care, leaving him his books.

characters.name: Dan Suggs
characters.role: Leader of the Suggs brothers, horse thieves and back-shooters Jake falls in with — the plain, banal face of murder.
characters.fate: Hanged by Call and Gus in Kansas, cursing everyone in reach.

characters.name: Dee Boot
characters.role: Flash gambler-gunman, Elmira's old flame, the rumor she chases a thousand miles up the plains.
characters.fate: Hanged in Ogallala for shooting a settler boy — days after Elmira finally reaches him.

characters.name: Big Zwey
characters.role: Enormous, gentle, slow buffalo hunter who falls dumbly in love with Elmira and freights her across the plains.
characters.fate: Dies defending her from the Sioux raiding party east of Ogallala.

characters.name: Xavier Wanz
characters.role: Fastidious proprietor of the Dry Bean saloon, weeping in his dishrag for love of Lorena.
characters.fate: After Lorena leaves, burns the Dry Bean down around himself, in her room.

characters.name: Allen O'Brien
characters.role: The elder Irish brother, who sings the lament over Sean's grave on the Nueces.
characters.fate: Rides the whole trail to Montana.

characters.name: Bill Spettle
characters.role: One of the two silent Spettle brothers, hired on with their thirty-dollar horses. Seventeen years old.
characters.fate: Killed by lightning in a storm-driven stampede on the trail north.
